issues.title: After configuring multiple types of mcp-server in mcp-client, an error is reported: Failed to instantiate [java.util.List]: Factory method 'mcpSyncClients' threw exception with message: Failed to wait for the message endpoint
issues.body:
> **Bug description**

After configuring multiple types of mcp-server in mcp-client, an error is reported: Failed to instantiate [java.util.List]: Factory method 'mcpSyncClients' threw exception with message: Failed to wait for the message endpoint

The project configuration is as follows:

spring:
>ai:
>>zhipuai:
>>>api-key: myKey
>>>>chat:
>>>>>options:
>>>>>>model: glm-4-flash-250414

>>mcp:
>>>client:
>>>>enabled: true
>>>>name: soldier-client
>>>>type: SYNC
>>>>sse:
>>>>>connections:
>>>>>>soldier-server:
>>>>>>>url: http://localhost:9526/soldier
>>>>>baidu-map:
>>>>>>>url: https://mcp.map.baidu.com/sse?ak=myKey

**Environment**
SpringBoot:3.4.2
SpringAI: 1.0.0-M8
jdk17
Soldier-server is available normally. After adding baidu-map, an error will be reported when starting the project

comments.body:
This message comes from the underlying java SDK: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/java-sdk

What it does: During connection there's a lock, so you're not able to send a message. If you try to send a message during the connection phase, it waits for 10s. If the connection to the MCP server is not established within that time the error is thrown.

Are you sure that you can connect with your API key to the `baidu-map` endpoint? You can verify this by using the mcp inspector: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector

@denniskawurek I can use Cherry Studio client to connect to this MCP server normally, but I feel that there is a problem with my MCP server path. How should I configure the authentication key? Directly configuring on the URL should be incorrect, do you know?

@hbsjz-swl I don't have access to my computer now, but you could try to use the `connections.[name].sse-endpoint` property like it's described here: https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/mcp/mcp-client-boot-starter-docs.html#_sse_transport_properties

Set this value to /sse?ak=abc
And set the URL property to the other value.

But iirc there were recently some changes regarding the logic for the URL builder, so I'm unsure whether it's already release. I think you need to try it out. :)

> [@hbsjz-swl](https://github.com/hbsjz-swl) I don't have access to my computer now, but you could try to use the `connections.[name].sse-endpoint` property like it's described here: https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/mcp/mcp-client-boot-starter-docs.html#_sse_transport_properties
> 
> Set this value to /sse?ak=abc And set the URL property to the other value.
> 
> But iirc there were recently some changes regarding the logic for the URL builder, so I'm unsure whether it's already release. I think you need to try it out. :)

Thank you very much for your guidance. I tried it but it still didn't work. I switched to version 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT to solve this problem.
